_readme: PLUNGE SEO KEYWORD LIBRARY — private working inventory. Lives in _generator/
on purpose: this folder is NEVER published (netlify.toml publishes only
docs/). NEVER move or copy this file into docs/ — it would become publicly
downloadable and hand competitors the whole keyword strategy.
Built 2026-07-14 by merging (deduplicated, 1,024 unique keywords):
1. The owner's Google Keyword Planner export (725 keywords, Jul 2025-Jun 2026)
as scored/categorized by the Cowork analysis (keyword-analysis-plunge.pdf +
plunge-keyword-strategy.xlsx, both on the owner's Desktop).
2. 279 NEW local city×service keywords (the export's 'local gap' — it had
exactly 1 Arizona geo term). Evidence: Google Autocomplete patterns.
3. 20 NEW question keywords mapped to future blog posts (the 'question gap').
PRIORITY SCORE (0-100, scored_export only): 45% volume tier + 35% inverse
ads-competition + 20% bid value. Two caveats from the analysis: volumes are
NATIONAL and bucketed (50/500/5k/50k/500k) — treat as relative, not literal;
'competition' measures AD auctions, not organic difficulty. New local/blog
keywords carry no volume numbers (Google doesn't publish city-level volumes
for free) — their demand is evidenced by live autocomplete.
in_use_where = automated verbatim scan of services.json copy + blog-posts.json
content at build time. Mechanical usage (titles/H1s/metas like 'Drain Cleaning
in Mesa, AZ') is NOT flagged here — those cover their patterns by construction.
ACTION PLAN (from the Cowork PDF, condensed):
- Before launch: fold Tier 1 local keywords into existing titles/H1s/metas.
- Launch week: Google Search Console + sitemap (free replacement for paid tools).
- Month 1-2: create 3 flagged service pages (tankless installation, slab leak
repair, repipe); blog batch #1 = polybutylene-pipes + water-heater posts.
- Month 3+: read Search Console monthly; optimize pages at positions 5-15.
- Ongoing: blog batches per blog plan; refresh this analysis twice a year (free).
HIGHLIGHT: 'polybutylene pipes' — 5,000 nat'l searches, LOW competition, perfect
fit for 1980s-90s AZ homes, almost nobody targets it locally.
